disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent atraumatic subluxations of the left shoulder and right knee over about four hours, each spontaneously reducing; no joint currently remains out of place or deformed.
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me exactly what happened with the joints?
Did the joints go back into place, or is something still out of place?
Does either joint look deformed or out of shape right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: They kind of popped out and then slid back in on their own, both the shoulder and the knee. Nothing's stuck out of place now and they don't look deformed — it's the cramping that's unbearable.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No numbness, pins-and-needles, weakness, coldness, or change in colour of the affected hand or foot — patient-observable distal neurovascular screen is negative.
trigger_question_examples: Are your fingers or toes numb, tingling, weak, cold, or a different colour?
Can you feel and move your hand and your foot normally?
Is the skin on your hand or foot pale, blue, or cold to touch?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my fingers and toes feel totally normal — I can move them fine, they're not numb or cold, and the colour looks normal.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I haven't really checked, I've just been focused on the pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, and no single hot, red, markedly swollen joint; patient not systemically unwell — septic arthritis screen negative.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any fever, or is one joint hot, red and very swollen?
Have you felt feverish or generally unwell in yourself?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, and nothing's hot or red — it's more the muscles cramping than the joint being swollen. I don't feel sick in myself otherwise.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established diagnosis of hypermobile Ehlers-Danlos syndrome with frequent prior subluxations and early secondary osteoarthritis of the affected joints.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any medical conditions I should know about?
Has this happened before, or do you have a joint or connective-tissue condition?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, I've got hypermobile EDS — my joints are really loose and slip out easily. I've been told I've got early arthritis in this shoulder and knee too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Patient is immobilised on the floor, lives alone, and cannot safely transfer or reach food and water.
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to move safely, and is there anyone there with you?
Can you get yourself up, and do you have help at home if you need it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I'm stuck on the floor, I live on my own, and I can't get up to reach the kitchen or get any water.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No effective analgesia is available in the home at present.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any pain relief at home you could take right now?
What have you already tried for the pain today?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got nothing here that helps — there are no painkillers in the house at the moment.
